summary: Security review accepts the M4 Run Scan API and orchestrator slice. POST input uses a strict Zod schema with bounded ids and limits, API error responses are sanitized and consistent with the existing route style, SearchRun error messages redact common token/API-key patterns and are length-bounded, the default scan path creates no LLM client or real SERP provider and has no fetch/process.env/lib/env usage, useMockSerp false fails closed without an injected provider, and database writes use Prisma structured operations within the expected local unauthenticated MVP scope.

security_checklist.notes: runScanInputSchema is strict, requires non-empty radarTaskId capped at 191 characters, defaults useMockSerp to true, and bounds keywordLimit and serpLimit to integers from 1 to 50.

Invalid JSON, validation, not-found, inactive-task, unsupported-provider, and internal errors return generic structured JSON without ids, stack traces, Prisma details, provider details, or raw runtime messages.

Thrown error messages are normalized, redacted for common sk-token/Bearer/key-token-secret patterns, capped at 240 characters per error, and summarized into SearchRun.errorMessage with a 1000-character cap.
Product implementation files do not import lib/env or read process.env. README references are documentation only; tests include fake secret redaction assertions.

security_checklist.item: No network or paid service by default
security_checklist.notes: The default orchestrator path uses MockSerpProvider and no LLM client. Focused tests block global fetch for the default scan path.
Without an injected provider, useMockSerp false throws RunScanUnsupportedProviderError before creating a SearchRun, so the API fails closed instead of falling through to a real provider.

The Prisma repository uses structured create/update calls only, no raw SQL, no schema or migration edits, and records partial failures without deleting successful keyword, SERP, or opportunity writes.
The slice does not add ad hoc auth/session/cron behavior and README records auth/session changes as a non-goal for this local MVP slice.
Reviewed product changes are inside the task allowed paths. Dirty project coordination files are treated as workflow state, not product implementation scope.

tests_run.result: PASS: 8 focused scan/API tests passed, including default no-network mock scan, invalid/inactive/unsupported preconditions, partial failure, failed-run sanitized errors, and API response shapes.

tests_run.command: rg -n 'process\.env|lib/env|fetch\(|OpenAICompatibleClient|executeRaw|queryRaw|\$transaction' services/scan-orchestrator.ts app/api/scans/run lib/schemas.ts tests/scan-orchestrator.test.ts tests/run-scan-api.test.ts README.md
tests_run.result: PASS: no product implementation hits for process.env, lib/env, fetch, OpenAICompatibleClient, raw SQL, or transactions. Hits were README documentation only.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed line-ending conversion warnings for README.md, lib/schemas.ts, and project coordination JSON files.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully. Existing nonfatal local SWC native-package warnings were printed before fallback build continued.
PASS: security reviewer handoff JSON validates.

decisions: Accepted the default local deterministic path because it uses MockSerpProvider, does not construct OpenAICompatibleClient or real SERP providers, and has no product fetch usage.
Accepted useMockSerp false behavior because the production API cannot inject a provider and the orchestrator throws before creating a run when no provider is supplied.
Accepted the existing API style of logging unexpected server errors while returning generic INTERNAL_ERROR responses; no configured secrets are read by this slice.
Accepted Prisma structured partial writes because partial_failed is an explicit task requirement and successful opportunities should survive single-keyword failures.
risks: The POST route remains intentionally unauthenticated for the local single-admin MVP; production exposure still requires the later auth/session gate and deployment policy.

Run result errors and SearchRun.errorMessage intentionally retain keyword and stage context after sanitizing thrown error messages; avoid putting secrets into Radar Task content or generated keyword text.
An explicitly injected SerpProvider or LlmClient can perform external work by design for tests/future wiring; this review accepts only the default production API path for no-network/no-paid behavior.
requested_action: Proceed with QA review for M4-RUN-SCAN-API-001. Security review accepts the implementation with local-MVP residual risks noted.
